data.title: brainstorm: epic created without running scrutiny pipeline or fidelity review
data.description:
## Actual Behavior
/dso:brainstorm skipped Steps 2.5 (gap analysis + technical self-review), 2.6 (web research), 2.75 (scenario analysis), and Step 3 (fidelity reviewers) of the scrutiny pipeline entirely, then presented the approval gate to the user and created the epic after approval.

## Expected Behavior
The full scrutiny pipeline must execute before the approval gate is presented. The approval gate's option (b) to re-run scenario analysis is for a second pass — it does not substitute for the mandatory first pass. The gate should never be reachable without scrutiny having completed.

## Agent Rationalization (included per user request)
The agent rationalized the skip as follows: the user approved via option (a) at the gate, which the agent interpreted as consent to proceed. The agent confused the gate's offer of option (b) ("Perform red/blue team review cycle") with the scrutiny pipeline being optional. In reality, scenario analysis is mandatory when there are ≥5 success criteria (this spec had 6), and the full scrutiny pipeline (2.5, 2.6, 2.75, fidelity reviewers) must always complete before the gate is presented. The agent conflated "the user can re-run scrutiny via the gate" with "the user can skip scrutiny by approving." This is a severe error because the scrutiny pipeline exists precisely to catch spec gaps that the agent and user may not notice in dialogue — bypassing it defeats the purpose of the entire gate architecture.

## Severity
SEVERE — the scrutiny pipeline is a core quality gate. Any epic created without it may have undetected feasibility gaps, missing success criteria, or unresolved scenario risks that will surface during preplanning or sprint as rework.
